definition: The capacity to recover quickly from difficulties; toughness; the ability to spring back.
example: Resilience is believing that when things are bad, you can still change them.

definition: A return to a former prosperous or successful state; a recovery after a setback.
example: A true comeback begins when you know what you are fighting for—and refuse to stay down.

definition: The state or quality of being worthy of honor or respect; self-respect.
example: Dignity insists we live in a country great enough to help a man when he is in trouble.

definition: Steady persistence in a course of action in spite of difficulties, obstacles, or discouragement.
example: Perseverance answers danger with resolve: you think boxing is dangerous? So is giving up.

definition: A group consisting of parents and children living together; all the descendants of a common ancestor.
example: Family made him everybody’s hope, the kids’ hero, and the champion of one heart above all.

definition: A feeling of expectation and desire for a certain thing to happen; grounds for believing that something good may happen.
example: Hope is the belief that when things are bad, you can still change them.

definition: A person who has defeated or surpassed all rivals in a competition; a person who fights for a cause.
example: You are the champion of my heart, James J. Braddock.

definition: Courage and resolve; strength of character; firmness of mind or spirit in the face of adversity.
example: Grit is knowing what you are fighting for when the odds say walk away.

definition: To accept as true; to have confidence in.
example: I have to believe that when things are bad I can change them.

definition: To take part in a violent struggle; to struggle determinedly.
example: I know what I’m fighting for—and that knowledge is heavier than any opponent.

definition: Financial or material assistance; alleviation of distress.
example: Dignity asked for work, not pity—yet relief kept a family standing until the comeback.

definition: A boxer of the heaviest class; metaphorically, a major challenge.
example: A heavyweight title is loud; a heavyweight character is quiet and rarer.

definition: A feeling of needing food; strong desire or craving.
example: Hunger can mean dinner—or the sharper hunger to reclaim your name.

definition: An assurance that one will do something; grounds for expectation.
example: He fought on a promise to his children that the lights would stay on.

definition: A firmly held belief or opinion.
example: Conviction without evidence is noise; conviction after struggle is leadership.

definition: The quality of being coherent and intelligible.
example: Clarity is kindness in speech—confusion is often a way to hide.
